name: VR Walk the Plank – Between the Towers

reviews.review_description: Best game ever I loved it
My six-year-old was very disappointed with this game. It was not scary at all! And we can’t even return it. Waste of money!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback.

If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

This is not a traditional game — there are no levels, no scoring, no gamification.
It’s a pure VR experience about how your body responds to height.
 
When you stand up and take a few steps onto the plank, your body reacts — even though you know it’s not real.
That’s the whole point.

And it becomes even more interesting as a shared experience:
Watching others, encouraging each other, and feeling how everyone reacts differently — it turns into a powerful, surprising group moment.

https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

reviews.review_description: It’s a mediocre scan of a building with a photo in the background.

developer_response.body:
If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

“Fight the Plank” isn’t a game in the usual sense.
No levels, no controller, no big effects — just you, the plank, and one clear question:
Do you dare to stand up, look down — and move across the plank?

This experience starts in your body.
Once you’re standing, you feel your balance shift, your mind hesitate — even though you know you’re safe.
Just a few steps, and your body takes over.
It works for everyone, no matter how experienced they are with VR.

And that’s exactly why people share it.
Because once you’ve felt it yourself, you want to see it in others — the hesitation, the laughter, the surprise.
Everyone reacts differently — but no one walks away untouched.
That’s Fight the Plank.

https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

reviews.review_description:
If you like wasting you money to look a picture of you floating in the air looking at a blurry image and a plank this is the game for you 😁

IT SUCKS, I wasted my money to look at it for 2 seconds and regret my life decisions and delete it right away give me a refund bro!!

developer_response.body:
You might want to check the review by Phoenix just above – they describe exactly how the app works and why it’s so effective.

This is not a game, and it’s not a still image. This VR experience only works while standing: When you actually stand up and take a few steps onto the plank, your brain gets tricked – even though you know you’re safe, your body reacts with genuine discomfort.

If you try it while sitting and only glance at it briefly, you’ll miss the entire effect.

Here’s a short clip showing how strongly people react:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

It’s often even more fun with friends – one person stands up while the others watch. Maybe give it another try and let your group take a turn.

reviews.review_description: This is not a game, u just stand thier turning left or right. No movement, just a view and thats it..........WHY Lie in the Description..?  .....WHY???

developer_response.body:
If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

You’re right — this isn’t a game in the usual sense. There’s nothing to control, no progress bar, no levels. And that’s exactly the point.

What happens here doesn’t play out on a screen — it happens in your body.
The moment you stand up and look down, your instincts take over.
It works on everyone. It’s not about what you press — it’s about what you feel.

And because that reaction is so strong, people immediately want to show it to others.
Someone tries it, then the next person wants to feel it too.
There’s laughter, hesitation, cheering — suddenly, a solo VR moment becomes a shared group experience.

People pass it on not because it’s a game, but because they want to see how others react.

👉 https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

reviews.review_description:
The person hired to do the description earned their money.

There is no experience in this app. Simply stood on a plank to look at the one continuous view. Can’t move, can’t change view, nothing. 

Quite the achievement to not be worth £1.

developer_response.body:
This app doesn’t rely on controls – it relies on your body.

There’s nothing to press, no view to switch – that’s exactly the point. You stand still, look down – and your body starts to react. Many users report trembling, sweating, even physical blocks. That’s the real experience: feeling how VR can trigger the nervous system, even when the mind knows it’s not real.

If you’re looking for a classic game, this isn’t it. But if you’re curious how your body handles height, space, and self-overcoming, you’ll be surprised how real it feels.

Tip: Read the review by phoenix0870 or watch this short video to understand what it’s really about:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼

If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

“Fight the Plank” isn’t a game in the usual sense – it’s a physical experience.
The effect starts the moment you stand up, look down, and face the height. If you stay seated, you miss the whole point.

This app triggers a real body reaction in nearly everyone – even though your brain knows it’s not real, your body doesn’t care.
That’s why so many people show it to friends: to see how they react. That’s the real thrill.
 
Maybe you just didn’t stand up. And if you did – respect.
Here’s what it looks like when it works:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼

reviews.review_description:
I found this VR app and it's description intrigued me and gave it a try. 

Immersed in the captivating scenery you move on the plank and there's a thing:
The overwhelming, entrapped visual perception takes over, overrides all other senses and gives you a shiver of fear by virtually realizing the altitude perceived and real, and how narrow the plank supporting your steps is. Family members observing my cautious and awkward movements in the room laughed at it and wanted to know what was going on in 'my head' with the VR goggles ;))

I had a thought to swap the roles with the family members and asked them to try the challenge. We had a lot of fun at each other's charge ;))

When I have decided to write this review, I have seen some negative ones in place. I read them first and wondered, did these people understand how to use the app? Strange...

developer_response.body:
What a wonderful review – thank you so much!
You captured exactly what this experience is about: It’s not a game in the classic sense, but a perceptual experiment. That moment when your senses contradict what your mind knows – and yet it all feels completely real – that’s the core of it.

Especially since you mentioned that some users might have misunderstood the app:
Yes, you really have to stand up and move – only then does the immersive effect kick in. The body reacts instinctively, and it often surprises not just the person in VR, but everyone watching too.

Here’s a real-life recording of someone trying it out:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

Thanks again for sharing – and best regards to your whole family!

reviews.review_description: I was so excited to try this. It looked awesome. However after trying to fit first bridge in my boundary for like 30 minutes I finally gave up. I set up boundary for 40 ft straight so I can walk one bridge and it still don’t work. You can move bridge little bit to adjust to your boundary, but only one way. Anyhow. Not sure if it’s just me, or this is crap.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!
You only need one or two meters of space – nothing more. The virtual plank can be rotated, so you can easily adjust it to your room layout. Just stand where you have space to take a few steps forward and back. No need to rearrange or move furniture.

The key isn’t the space – it’s how your body reacts: You stand up, look down – and your body instantly responds with real vertigo. That’s what makes this experience so unique.

What makes it even more powerful is the group dynamic: friends and family often watch you take on the challenge, film the moment, and share it. It becomes a social experience that sparks laughter and conversation.

👉 https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼

If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

reviews.review_description: This app just gives you a view high above between two buildings. It’s not a plank app or anything similar. There is no movement. It doesn’t move with your movements or with any controllers etc. Just a view! Load of trash. Doesn’t waste your money on this.

developer_response.body:
This is not a controller game, not a simulation, and not about movement mechanics. The concept is simple: you stand up, look down – and your body reacts. That’s the whole experience.

User “phoenix0870” described it perfectly in their review: “We were laughing with friends even though we knew it wasn’t real.”
That’s the point: The reaction happens in your mind, not through the software.

Whether you try it alone or let others watch – the effect is physical, immediate, and real.
See it here:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

reviews.review_description: Total bull. It doesn’t do anything. Only a picture. Refund would be appreciated.

developer_response.body:
If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

Sorry to hear it didn’t work for you as expected. Fight the Plank isn’t about buttons or movement on a joystick — the experience starts when you stand up, look down, and physically step forward. That’s when the height reaction kicks in.

It’s not a game with actions or missions — it’s a body-based experience. Most users are surprised how real it feels once they move. Try it while standing — and you’ll see why people love showing it to others.

👉 https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

reviews.review_description: Literally nothing to do. So so so so bad. Why is not this free?

developer_response.body:
If you’re curious about how this app really feels – check out the review by phoenix0870. It captures the experience better than we ever could.

This app isn’t about doing tasks – it’s about how your body responds.
You stand up, look down – and suddenly, something kicks in. Not because you’re in danger, but because it feels real.

“Fight the Plank” isn’t a classic game. No levels, no goals. It’s about that one moment of physical uncertainty,
when your brain says “it’s fine” – but your body isn’t so sure.

That’s why people keep sharing it. They want to see how others react: the hesitation, the laughter, the shift in balance.
If you’ve felt it, you know – this is not about action. It’s about perception.

We don’t know if you actually stood up. But if you’re curious how others deal with the experience, watch this:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼

reviews.review_description: I  my opinion this is an absolute load of tosh. The person who did the write up for the promotion did an amazing job as it convinced me to buy  i wish I hadn't bothered. It starts up and your just on a rooftop looking around. You can't move or do anything else despite pressing all buttons on controllers. Has to press meta button then. Select quit to close it. Uninstalled.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! Our app is designed to give you a true sense of height and challenge you to walk across a narrow plank suspended 50 meters above the ground. The thrill comes from actually moving in your space – no teleporting or controller navigation – so you can feel the heights up close!

Here’s a pro tip for even more fun: try it with friends! They can film you on their phones as you walk the plank and face your fear of heights for the first time. It makes for an unforgettable video that you can share with #fighttheplank. For anyone looking to experience VR in a new, active way and push their own limits, our app offers the ultimate adventure. Go ahead, take the first step – the thrill is real!

reviews.review_description: First game I tried and the cookies kept popping up. When the VR screen showed up after taking my surrounding 3D Match the "plank" kept shifting. Lame game.

developer_response.body:
Reviews like Phoenix’s make it very clear what this app is really about – and why it can feel so intense.

The effect doesn’t happen while sitting down. You need to stand up and actually step toward the plank. Your body reacts, even though your brain knows you’re safe – that’s the whole experience.

If the plank seems to be “shifting,” it’s usually due to the Guardian setup. Please recalibrate on a flat surface – then it will stay steady.

You can see how strong the reactions can be here:
https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr

Best to try with friends – one steps forward, the others watch. It often becomes the most surprising moment of the evening.

reviews.review_description: Just a picture of a plank. No other scenarios, no fall physics.

developer_response.body:
This app isn’t about simulating how to fall – it’s about showing how powerfully your body reacts to a simple illusion.

The effect works because it’s just a plank. You stand, look down — and suddenly your knees shake, your pulse rises, and your body hesitates. Even though you know you’re safe. There are no buttons, no levels — just you and the illusion. That’s the whole point.

With friends, the experience becomes even stronger: Who dares to step first? Who freezes? Who can’t move at all? That shared tension is part of the fun — it’s perfect for groups. And it’s often just as thrilling to watch others take the challenge.
 
Check out the review from phoenix0870 — or just watch the video. It immediately shows what this app is really about:

https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼

reviews.review_description: Very realistic. Bit unnerving at first,seems you start on the plank in the middle between two towers. Graphics very impressive  very good impression of being up there. ,with the feeling of height only thing needed really is a menu after the foreword advisory. But well worth the money. 👍👍👍

developer_response.body:
Hi DavE,
Thank you for your fantastic and constructive feedback! We're delighted that you enjoyed our #FightTheBlank VR experience and appreciate the realistic representation.

We want to assure you that the initial experience, where you find yourself in the middle of the plank and the fear of heights sets in, is precisely as intended. We aim to make you feel the adrenaline rush right from the start and provide an impressive sense of height - that's the thrill we want to offer.

The fun and experience become even more significant when you share it with your friends and observe their reactions. It's a great way to collectively enjoy exciting moments and create memories.

We are continuously working on #FightTheBlank and have plans to add many more exciting features. Your feedback is valuable to us and assists us in making the app even better.

Stay tuned; there are many thrilling updates on the way!

reviews.review_description: The plank should be narrower and maybe some animations below, otherwise a good app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We're glad to hear you’re enjoying the app and finding it thrilling. The challenge of overcoming your fear of heights is exactly what we aim for – and for many users, the current setup is already enough to muster the courage to step out onto the plank!

We take it as a huge compliment that you’d like an even narrower plank and additional effects below to heighten the sense of depth. For those looking to take the thrill even further, we’re always open to exploring new ways to enhance the experience. Thanks for the great ideas!

reviews.review_description: My first game I tried to purchase. Complete waste of time and money.

developer_response.body:
If you were expecting a classic game with scores, levels, or mechanics — then this might not match your expectations. But that’s exactly the point:

This VR experience isn’t a game loop — it’s a physical challenge. The moment your brain knows it’s not real, but your body reacts as if it is — that’s the core of it.

It works especially well in a group setting: watching friends, laughing together, daring each other — that’s where the magic happens. Some users even enjoy just watching others take the challenge.

You’ll get a better sense by checking out the video — or reading the review by phoenix0870, who really nails it.

https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16nmvfZ6ar/?mibextid=wwXIfr￼
